dq_rules.proposed_action: Deduplicate: keep the last occurrence per employee_id.

business_rules.name: tenure_calculation
business_rules.description: Compute tenure_years as (exit_date or today) minus hire_date, in years.

business_rules.name: high_overtime_threshold
business_rules.description: Flag employees with overtime_hours_per_month > 15 as 'high overtime' for risk-factor analysis.

business_rules.name: engagement_score_categories
business_rules.description: Categorize engagement_score as 'High' for scores >= 4.5 and 'Low' for scores <= 3.5.

business_rules.name: recent_promotion_detection
business_rules.description: Detect employees with last_promotion_date within the last 2 years.

business_rules.name: attrition_risk_flag
business_rules.description: Flag employees with attrition_flag as True for risk-factor analysis.

Here are the proposed cleaning rules explained in 2-3 plain-English sentences for an HR stakeholder:

**Rule 1: Deduplicate employee IDs**
We'll remove duplicate employee IDs to ensure we're working with unique records. If an employee ID is duplicated, we'll keep the last occurrence to maintain a complete record.

**Rule 2-5: Fill missing values**
We'll flag missing values in exit date, performance rating, last promotion date, and compensation columns as "Unknown" to prevent errors in analysis. This will help us maintain data consistency and make informed decisions.

**Rule 6-7: Standardize date formats**
We'll change the date formats in hire date, exit date, and last promotion date columns to a standard format (YYYY-MM-DD) to make it easier to analyze and compare data.

**Rule 8: Standardize department text**
We'll convert department names to lowercase to ensure consistency and make it easier to search and filter data.

These rules will help us clean and prepare the data for analysis, ensuring that our findings are accurate and reliable.

Applied 11 approved rule(s): ['dq-1 (dedupe on employee_id)', 'dq-2 (fill_unknown on exit_date)', 'dq-3 (standardize_date on exit_date)', 'dq-4 (impute_median on performance_rating)', 'dq-5 (fill_unknown on last_promotion_date)', 'dq-6 (fill_unknown on compensation)', 'dq-7 (fill_unknown on engagement_score)', 'dq-8 (standardize_text on department)', 'dq-9 (standardize_date on hire_date)', 'dq-10 (standardize_date on exit_date)', 'dq-11 (standardize_date on last_promotion_date)']. Silver now has 1200 rows -> 901372dc_silver.csv.

Here are the proposed business definitions explained in 2-3 plain sentences each:

**Top Performer Definition**
We define a "top performer" as an employee who has a performance rating of 4 or higher. This will help us identify employees who consistently meet or exceed expectations. 

**Tenure Calculation**
To calculate an employee's tenure, we will calculate the number of years between their hire date and the current date or their exit date, whichever is earlier. This will give us a clear picture of an employee's length of service.

**High Overtime Threshold**
We will flag employees who work more than 15 hours of overtime per month as "high overtime" for risk-factor analysis. This will help us identify employees who may be at risk of burnout or other issues.

**Engagement Score Categories**
We will categorize employee engagement scores as "High" for scores of 4.5 or higher and "Low" for scores of 3.5 or lower. This will help us understand the level of engagement among our employees.

**Recent Promotion Detection**
We will detect employees who have received a promotion within the last 2 years. This will help us identify employees who are being recognized for their hard work and may be at risk of leaving the company.

**Attrition Risk Flag**
We will flag employees who have left the company (attrition_flag = True) for risk-factor analysis. This will help us understand the reasons behind employee turnover and identify areas for improvement.
